item.title: LTIMindtree Ltd Sees Significant Open Interest Surge Amid Bearish Price Action
item.description: LTIMindtree Ltd (LTM), a large-cap player in the Computers - Software & Consulting sector, has witnessed a notable 12.75% increase in open interest in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity despite the stock’s recent underperformance. This surge in open interest, coupled with declining price trends and subdued investor participation, suggests evolving market positioning and potential directional bets among traders. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Shriram Finance Sees Notable Surge in Derivatives Open Interest Amid Mixed Price Action
item.description: Shriram Finance Ltd (SHRIRAMFIN) has witnessed a notable 11.04% increase in open interest (OI) in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity and shifting investor positioning. Despite a recent dip in share price and a downgrade in its Mojo Grade from Buy to Hold, the surge in OI alongside rising volumes suggests a complex interplay of directional bets and hedging strategies among traders. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: National Aluminium Company Ltd Sees High-Value Trading Amid Consecutive Declines
item.description: National Aluminium Company Ltd (NATIONALUM), a mid-cap player in the Non-Ferrous Metals sector, witnessed significant trading activity on 20 March 2026, with over 2 crore shares exchanging hands and a total traded value exceeding ₹7,395 crores. Despite this high liquidity and institutional interest, the stock has experienced a notable downward trend, falling 3.88% on the day and continuing a three-day losing streak that has eroded 8.33% of its value. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: ITC Ltd. Sees Exceptional Volume Surge Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: ITC Ltd., a heavyweight in the FMCG sector, witnessed one of the highest trading volumes on 20 March 2026, with over 2.69 crore shares exchanging hands. Despite a modest 1.96% gain on the day, the stock’s volume surge and technical indicators suggest a complex interplay of accumulation and distribution, warranting close attention from investors and market analysts alike. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Shows Steady Gains with Sectoral Divergence on 20 Mar 2026
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, demonstrated steady resilience on 20 Mar 2026, closing with a gain of 0.57%. This performance underscores the segment’s relative strength amid mixed market conditions, supported by broad-based sectoral contributions and a positive advance-decline ratio. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Petronet LNG Sees Sharp Open Interest Surge Amid Price Weakness
item.description: Petronet LNG Ltd., a mid-cap player in the gas sector, has witnessed a notable surge in open interest in its derivatives segment, even as the stock price continues to languish near a 52-week low. This divergence between rising market participation and declining price levels signals a complex positioning landscape that investors and traders should carefully analyse. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Sensex Edges Higher as Banking Sector Leads Amid Mixed Mid and Small Cap Performance
item.description: Indian equity markets closed with modest gains on 20 Mar 2026, as the Sensex advanced 230.82 points or 0.31% to settle at 74,438.06. The Nifty mirrored this positive momentum, supported by broad-based sectoral strength, particularly in the banking space. However, mid and small cap indices showed a more nuanced picture with selective sectoral performances and notable volatility among individual stocks. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Sees Mixed Momentum as JSW Steel Leads Gains and Hindalco Lags
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, recorded a modest gain of 0.37% on 20 Mar 2026, reflecting a cautious but positive market mood. While heavyweight stocks such as JSW Steel led the advance with a 3.20% return, others like Hindalco Industries lagged, posting a decline of 2.75%. The advance-decline ratio of 2.03x, with 67 stocks advancing against 33 declining, underscores a broadly constructive environment amid mixed sectoral trends. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Shows Resilience with Moderate Gains and Positive Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, as represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has demonstrated steady resilience with a modest gain of 0.42% on the day and a 0.23% rise over the past five sessions. Despite a mixed breadth and some technical call adjustments, the segment remains the best performer across market capitalisation categories, driven by notable stock-specific returns and sectoral dynamics. Time: 5 minutes ago

item.title: Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd Hits Intraday Low Amid Price Pressure
item.description: Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd (MCX) experienced a notable decline today, touching an intraday low of Rs 2,397.95, reflecting a 5.18% drop as the stock faced significant price pressure despite broader market gains. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: LTIMindtree Ltd Sees Sharp Open Interest Surge Amidst Mixed Market Signals
item.description: LTIMindtree Ltd (LTM) has witnessed a notable 11.99% increase in open interest in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity and evolving positioning among traders. Despite this surge, the stock underperformed its sector and broader indices, reflecting a complex interplay between investor sentiment and technical factors. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Multi Commodity Exchange Sees Sharp Open Interest Surge Amid Mixed Price Action
item.description: Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd (MCX) has witnessed a notable 10.3% surge in open interest in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity despite the stock’s recent underperformance. This increase in open interest, coupled with volume patterns and price movements, suggests evolving market positioning and potential directional bets among traders. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Shriram Finance Sees Significant Open Interest Surge Amid Mixed Market Signals
item.description: Shriram Finance Ltd, a prominent large-cap player in the Non Banking Financial Company (NBFC) sector, has witnessed a notable 10.5% surge in open interest (OI) in its derivatives segment, signalling increased market activity and shifting investor positioning. Despite a modest decline in the stock price, the rise in OI alongside volume patterns suggests evolving directional bets that merit close attention from market participants. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: ITC Ltd. Sees Robust Trading Activity Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: ITC Ltd., a heavyweight in the FMCG sector, emerged as one of the most actively traded stocks by value on 20 March 2026, with a total traded volume exceeding 2.09 crore shares and a turnover of ₹64,248.97 lakhs. Despite a strong intraday performance marked by a 2.82% gain, the stock faces a cautious outlook from institutional investors, reflected in its recent downgrade from Hold to Sell by MarketsMOJO on 9 February 2026. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Petronet LNG Sees Sharp Open Interest Surge Amid Bearish Price Action
item.description: Petronet LNG Ltd. has witnessed a notable 12.9% increase in open interest in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity despite the stock hitting a fresh 52-week low. This surge in open interest, coupled with declining prices and rising delivery volumes, suggests a complex interplay of investor positioning and potential directional bets in the gas sector. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Inox Green Energy Services Ltd Surges on Exceptional Volume Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: Inox Green Energy Services Ltd (INOXGREEN) witnessed a remarkable surge in trading volume on 20 Mar 2026, emerging as one of the most actively traded stocks in the Other Utilities sector. Despite a strong intraday price rally of over 15%, the stock’s technical indicators and investor participation reveal a complex picture, prompting a cautious outlook from market analysts. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Sensex Advances 0.79% Led by Banking and Steel; Mid and Small Caps Also Gain
item.description: The Indian equity market witnessed a broad-based rally on 20 Mar 2026, with the Sensex climbing 583.67 points or 0.79% to close at 74,790.91. Midcaps and small caps also participated in the uptrend, supported by strong sectoral performances and positive global cues. However, select sectors such as Realty and Banking lagged, reflecting mixed investor sentiment amid ongoing macroeconomic considerations. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Sees Mixed Momentum as JSW Steel Leads Gains and HDFC Bank Lags
item.description: The large-cap segment demonstrated a cautiously optimistic performance on 20 Mar 2026, with the BSE 100 index rising by 0.87%. While heavyweight stocks such as JSW Steel delivered robust returns, defensive stalwarts like HDFC Bank faced downward pressure, reflecting a nuanced market environment marked by divergent sectoral trends and selective investor appetite. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Shows Resilient Gains Amid Broad Market Advances
item.description: The small-cap segment, as measured by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has demonstrated steady resilience with a gain of 0.81% on 20 Mar 2026, extending its positive momentum over the past week with a 0.62% rise. This performance underscores the segment’s continued appeal amid a mixed market backdrop, supported by strong breadth and selective sectoral leadership. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Shines with 1.07% Gain Led by Escorts Kubota
item.description: The mid-cap segment demonstrated robust performance on 20 Mar 2026, with the BSE MIDCAP 150 index advancing by 1.07% on the day and registering a 0.83% gain over the past five trading sessions. This sustained upward momentum underscores renewed investor confidence in mid-sized companies, driven by select sectoral contributions and favourable breadth dynamics. Time: 1 hour ago
